next_steps: Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) for direct visualization of the gastric lumen
Psychiatric evaluation for underlying trichotillomania or trichophagia
Obtain a detailed history regarding hair pulling or eating habits
Surgical consultation if a large bezoar is confirmed to assess need for retrieval
Maintain intravenous fluids and electrolyte stabilization
Esophagogastroduodenoscopy (EGD) to evaluate for gastric bezoar, peptic ulcer, or mucosal inflammation
Urinary porphobilinogen and delta-aminolevulinic acid (ALA) to screen for acute porphyria
Comprehensive metabolic panel including inflammatory markers (ESR, CRP)
Detailed clinical history regarding hair-pulling habits or ingestion of non-food items
